message:
feat(core,ui,logging): implement message threading UI, mark-read functionality, enhanced Rich logging, and auto-register agents ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ MOTIVATION & PROBLEM STATEMENT ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ This commit implements four major feature enhancements that significantly improve the usability, visual feedback, and workflow efficiency of MCP Agent Mail: CATEGORY 1: MESSAGE THREADING - GMAIL-STYLE CONVERSATION VIEW ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Problem 1A: No Cohesive Thread Visualization ────────────────────────────────────────── The system had thread_id support in the database and displayed thread IDs in the UI, but there was NO way to actually view an entire thread as a conversation. Issues: 1. **Fragmented View**: Users saw thread_id tags like "#42" but clicking them did nothing. Each message displayed in isolation with no context. 2. **Lost Context**: When agents have multi-message conversations, users had to: - Manually search for related messages - Remember previous subjects - Piece together conversation flow from disparate inbox views - No chronological ordering across agents' inboxes 3. **Poor UX**: Thread indicators without thread views is a broken affordance: ```html Thread: #42 ``` This creates user frustration: "I can see there's a thread, why can't I view it?" 4. **Collaboration Confusion**: In multi-agent workflows: - Agent A sends message to Agent B (thread #42) - Agent B replies (thread #42) - Agent A sends follow-up (thread #42) Without thread view: - Each agent sees only messages TO them - No one sees the full conversation - Difficult to understand complete interaction history 5. **Debugging Difficulty**: When debugging agent interactions: - Need to see complete conversation flow - Current UI forces manual correlation of messages - Time-consuming to trace conversation threads Example Failure Scenario: ``` User debugging agent coordination issue: 1. Opens Agent A's inbox - sees "Task complete" message 2. Clicks thread_id "#42" - nothing happens 3. Manually searches for thread #42 4. Opens Agent B's inbox 5. Searches again for thread #42 6. Opens each message individually 7. Tries to mentally reconstruct chronological order 8. Gives up, wastes 10 minutes ``` Problem 1B: No Thread Entry Points ──────────────────────────────── Even with thread_id visible, there were no clickable links to access threads: - Message detail pages showed thread_id as plain text - Inbox views showed thread_id as decorative badge - No navigation path from "see thread exists" to "view thread" CATEGORY 2: MARK AS READ FUNCTIONALITY - INBOX MANAGEMENT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Problem 2A: Disabled Mark-Read Buttons (Placeholder UI) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── Previous commit (a5c3dd3) disabled several features with "Coming Soon" badges, including "Mark All Read" and selected message marking. Issues: 1. **Core Workflow Blocked**: Marking messages as read is ESSENTIAL for inbox management, not a "nice to have" feature 2. **Database Support Existed**: The `read_ts` column existed in message_recipients table, but no UI to actually USE it 3. **Inconsistent State**: Messages could be marked as read via MCP tool calls, but not through the web UI - creating confusion about feature availability 4. **User Frustration**: Users with 100+ unread messages had NO way to clear them: ```html ``` Problem 2B: No Batch Operations ───────────────────────────── Without mark-read functionality: - Users couldn't manage inbox state through UI - No way to mark multiple selected messages as read - No bulk "mark all" operation for inbox cleanup - Forces users to use MCP tool calls (command-line only) CATEGORY 3: LOGGING CLARITY - DEVELOPMENT EXPERIENCE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Problem 3A: Sparse Rich Logger Output ─────────────────────────────────── The existing Rich logger worked but provided minimal visual feedback: ``` ╭─ Tool: send_message ─╮ │ Project: test │ │ Duration: 123.45ms │ ╰───────────────────────╯ ``` Issues: 1. **Low Information Density**: Large panels with minimal information 2. **Poor Visual Hierarchy**: Everything looked equally important 3. **No Icons**: Text-only labels harder to scan quickly 4. **Bland Colors**: Minimal use of color for status differentiation 5. **No Performance Indicators**: Duration shown but not contextualized Problem 3B: Generic Status Reporting ────────────────────────────────── Status was shown as: ``` Status: ✓ SUCCESS ``` But no visual indication of: - Performance (fast vs slow) - Severity (success vs error) - Operation type (read vs write) - Critical vs routine operations CATEGORY 4: AGENT REGISTRATION FRICTION ───────────────────────────────────────── Problem 4A: Manual Registration Required ────────────────────────────────────── Previous default: `register_if_missing: bool = False` This meant: 1. **Extra Step**: Before sending first message, must manually register agent 2. **Error-Prone**: Easy to forget registration, leading to errors 3. **Friction**: Every new agent requires explicit registration call 4. **Poor DX**: Unnatural workflow (register then use, vs use auto-registers) Example frustrating workflow: ```python # Send message from new agent result = await client.call_tool("send_message", { "project": "test", "from": "new_agent", "to": ["recipient"], "subject": "Hello", "body": "Test" }) # ERROR: Agent 'new_agent' not found # Must stop, register agent await client.call_tool("register_agent", { "project": "test", "name": "new_agent", "program": "...", "model": "..." }) # Now try again result = await client.call_tool("send_message", {...}) # SUCCESS ``` Better workflow: ```python # Just send message result = await client.call_tool("send_message", { "project": "test", "from": "new_agent", # Auto-registers! "to": ["recipient"], "subject": "Hello", "body": "Test" }) # SUCCESS immediately ``` ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ CATEGORY 1: MESSAGE THREADING IMPLEMENTATION ────────────────────────────────────────────── File: src/mcp_agent_mail/http.py Added Three New HTTP Endpoints: ──────────────────────────────── **1. Thread View Endpoint** (GET /mail/{project}/thread/{thread_id}): ```python @fastapi_app.get("/mail/{project}/thread/{thread_id}", response_class=HTMLResponse) async def mail_thread(project: str, thread_id: str) -> HTMLResponse: """Display all messages in a thread chronologically (Gmail-style conversation view). NOTE: Currently loads ALL messages in thread without pagination. For threads with 1000+ messages, consider adding LIMIT/OFFSET pagination. """ ``` Implementation details: - Queries ALL messages where `thread_id` matches OR message ID matches - Orders messages chronologically (oldest first) for natural conversation flow - Handles both integer and string thread_ids - Loads complete thread (no pagination - potential performance consideration) SQL Query: ```sql SELECT m.id, m.subject, m.body_md, s.name, m.created_ts, m.importance, m.thread_id FROM messages m JOIN agents s ON s.id = m.sender_id WHERE m.project_id = :pid AND (m.thread_id = :tid OR m.id = :tid_int) -- Thread OR thread starter ORDER BY m.created_ts ASC ``` Why include `m.id = :tid_int`: - First message in thread has `thread_id = NULL` - Other messages reference first message's ID as their thread_id - Must include both to show complete thread **2. Mark Selected Messages Read** (POST /mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-read): ```python @fastapi_app.post("/mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-read") async def mark_selected_messages_read(project: str, agent: str, request: Request): """Mark specific messages as read for an agent.""" ``` Features: - Accepts array of message IDs in JSON body - Validates input (non-empty, max 500 messages) - Uses parameterized SQL to prevent injection - Returns count of actually marked messages - Handles race conditions (already-read messages) Request format: ```json { "message_ids": [123, 456, 789] } ``` Response format: ```json { "success": true, "marked_count": 3, "requested_count": 3, "agent": "agent_name", "project": "project_slug" } ``` SQL Implementation: ```python # Build parameterized query with placeholders placeholders = ','.join([f':mid{i}' for i in range(len(message_ids))]) params = {"aid": aid, "now": now} params.update({f"mid{i}": mid for i, mid in enumerate(message_ids)}) await session.execute( text(f""" UPDATE message_recipients SET read_ts = :now WHERE agent_id = :aid AND message_id IN ({placeholders}) AND read_ts IS NULL """), params ) ``` Safety features: - Max 500 messages (prevents SQL parameter overflow - SQLite limit is 999) - Only updates unread messages (`read_ts IS NULL`) - Transaction safety with commit - Error handling with rollback **3. Mark All Messages Read** (POST /mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-all-read): ```python @fastapi_app.post("/mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-all-read") async def mark_all_messages_read(project: str, agent: str): """Mark all messages for an agent as read.""" ``` Simpler implementation: - No message ID array needed - Updates ALL unread messages for agent - Returns count of marked messages SQL: ```python UPDATE message_recipients SET read_ts = :now WHERE agent_id = :aid AND read_ts IS NULL ``` File: src/mcp_agent_mail/templates/mail_thread.html (NEW FILE) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Created entirely new template for Gmail-style thread view. Structure: ```html {% extends "base.html" %}

{{ thread_subject }}

Thread #{{ thread_id }} {{ message_count }} messages
{% for msg in messages %}
{% endfor %} ``` Design principles: 1. **Chronological**: Oldest → newest (natural conversation flow) 2. **Full Content**: Shows complete message bodies (not excerpts) 3. **Visual Hierarchy**: Alternating backgrounds, clear sender labels 4. **Markdown Support**: Renders body_md with proper formatting 5. **Metadata Rich**: Shows all message attributes (importance, timestamp, etc.) File: src/mcp_agent_mail/templates/mail_inbox.html ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── Thread Link Enhancement: BEFORE (Non-Clickable): ```html ``` AFTER (Clickable Link): ```html ``` Key improvements: 1. **Clickable**: Now an `` tag with `href` 2. **Styled**: Button-like appearance with hover states 3. **Labeled**: "Thread" text makes purpose clear 4. **Icon Updated**: Changed from `message-circle` to `messages-square` (more appropriate) 5. **Event Handling**: `@click.stop` prevents message row click handler from firing Mark-Read Implementation: Added two JavaScript functions to Alpine.js component: ```javascript async markSelectedAsRead() { if (this.selectedMessages.length === 0) return; const count = this.selectedMessages.length; if (!confirm(`Mark ${count} selected message${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} as read?`)) return; const response = await fetch(`/mail/${projectSlug}/inbox/${agentName}/mark-read`, { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({message_ids: this.selectedMessages}) }); const result = await response.json(); showToast(`Marked ${result.marked_count} as read`, 'success'); this.clearSelection(); setTimeout(() => window.location.reload(), 1000); } async markAllAsRead() { if (this.isRefreshing) return; if (!confirm('Mark all messages as read? This cannot be undone.')) return; this.isRefreshing = true; const response = await fetch(`/mail/${projectSlug}/inbox/${agentName}/mark-all-read`, { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, }); const result = await response.json(); if (result.marked_count === 0) { showToast('No unread messages to mark as read', 'info'); } else { showToast(`Marked ${result.marked_count} messages as read`, 'success'); setTimeout(() => window.location.reload(), 1500); } this.isRefreshing = false; } ``` Features: - Confirmation dialogs prevent accidental actions - Toast notifications provide feedback - Automatic page reload after success (shows updated read states) - Loading state prevents double-clicks (`isRefreshing` flag) - Fallback to `alert()` if toast function unavailable Button Changes: BEFORE (Disabled with "Soon" Badge): ```html ``` AFTER (Functional Button): ```html ``` Improvements: 1. **Functional**: Actually calls API endpoint 2. **Loading State**: Icon spins during API call 3. **Success Color**: Green background indicates positive action 4. **Disabled State**: Prevents double-clicks during processing 5. **No Badge**: Removed "Soon" badge (feature now live) Also changed color scheme: ```html class="bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-900/30 hover:bg-green-200 dark:hover:bg-green-800/40 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300" ``` Changed from `success-*` (custom classes) to `green-*` (standard Tailwind) for consistency with previous commit (a5c3dd3). File: src/mcp_agent_mail/templates/mail_message.html ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Thread Link Enhancement: BEFORE (Plain Text): ```html {% if message.thread_id %}
Thread: {{ message.thread_id }}
{% endif %} ``` AFTER (Call-to-Action Link): ```html {% if message.thread_id %} View Thread #{{ message.thread_id }} {% endif %} ``` Thread Context Card: BEFORE (Full Message List): ```html {% if thread_items %}
Thread Messages ({{ thread_items|length }})
{% for t in thread_items %}
{{ t.from }} {{ t.subject }} {{ t.created }}
{% endfor %}
{% endif %} ``` AFTER (Compact Preview with CTA): ```html {% if thread_items and thread_items|length > 1 %}

Part of a Conversation Thread

This message is part of a thread with {{ thread_items|length }} messages

View Full Thread
{% for t in thread_items[:5] %} {% if t.id != message.id %} {{ t.from }} {{ t.subject[:40] }} {% endif %} {% endfor %} {% if thread_items|length > 5 %} +{{ thread_items|length - 5 }} more {% endif %}
{% endif %} ``` Design improvements: 1. **Gradient Background**: Eye-catching primary color gradient 2. **Prominent CTA**: Large "View Full Thread" button 3. **Quick Preview**: Shows up to 5 other messages in thread 4. **Truncation**: Long subjects truncated to prevent overflow 5. **Conditional**: Only shows if thread has > 1 message (current message isn't alone) File: src/mcp_agent_mail/templates/mail_project.html ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Enhanced Header Actions with Human Overseer CTA: BEFORE (Icon-Only Buttons): ```html
``` AFTER (Prominent CTA + Secondary Buttons): ```html
Human Overseer
Reservations Attachments
``` Design improvements: 1. **Visual Hierarchy**: Human Overseer is primary CTA (gradient, larger) 2. **Animation**: Shimmer effect on hover draws attention 3. **Pulsing Dot**: Animated indicator suggests "action needed" 4. **Text Labels**: Secondary buttons now have text (not just icons) 5. **Clear Separation**: Divider separates primary from secondary actions 6. **Accessibility**: All buttons have clear text labels Rationale for prominence: - Human Overseer is important feature (human-in-the-loop) - Should be easily discoverable - High-priority messages from humans to agents - Deserves visual prominence over utility functions CATEGORY 2: ENHANCED RICH LOGGING ─────────────────────────────────── File: src/mcp_agent_mail/rich_logger.py Added Visual Enhancements to Tool Call Logging: **1. Icon-Based Labels**: BEFORE (Text Only): ``` Tool Name | send_message Timestamp | 2025-10-26 12:34:56 Project | test-project Agent | agent-1 Duration | 123.45ms Status | ✓ SUCCESS ``` AFTER (With Icons): ``` 🔧 Tool | send_message 🕐 Timestamp | 2025-10-26 12:34:56 📦 Project | test-project 🤖 Agent | agent-1 ⚡ Duration | 123.45ms ✅ Status | SUCCESS ``` Implementation: ```python table.add_row("🔧", "Tool", f"[bold bright_green]{ctx.tool_name}[/]") table.add_row("🕐", "Timestamp", f"[dim]{ctx.timestamp}[/dim]") table.add_row("📦", "Project", f"[bright_cyan]{escape(ctx.project)}[/]") table.add_row("🤖", "Agent", f"[bright_magenta]{escape(ctx.agent)}[/]") ``` Benefits: - Faster visual scanning (icons draw eye) - More engaging console output - Better information architecture - Easier to distinguish fields at a glance **2. Performance-Based Duration Coloring**: BEFORE (Static Thresholds): ```python if ctx.duration_ms < 100: color = "green" elif ctx.duration_ms < 1000: color = "yellow" else: color = "red" ``` AFTER (Granular with Icons): ```python if ctx.duration_ms < 50: duration_style = "bold bright_green" duration_icon = "⚡" # Lightning fast elif ctx.duration_ms < 100: duration_style = "bold green" duration_icon = "⚡" # Fast elif ctx.duration_ms < 500: duration_style = "bold yellow" duration_icon = "⏱" # Moderate elif ctx.duration_ms < 1000: duration_style = "bold bright_yellow" duration_icon = "⏱" # Slowish else: duration_style = "bold red" duration_icon = "🐌" # Slow! ``` Improvements: - More granular thresholds (5 levels instead of 3) - Icons provide instant visual feedback - Bright colors for extremes (very fast/very slow) - Snail emoji for slow operations (>1s) adds personality **3. Enhanced Panel Styling**: BEFORE: ```python Panel( syntax, title=f"[bold cyan]{title}[/bold cyan]", border_style="cyan", box=box.ROUNDED, ) ``` AFTER: ```python Panel( syntax, title=f"[bold bright_white]📥 {title}[/bold bright_white]", border_style="bright_blue", box=box.ROUNDED, theme="dracula", # Better syntax highlighting ) ``` Changes: - Icons in panel titles - Brighter colors (`bright_blue` vs `cyan`) - Dracula theme for better code highlighting - White titles for higher contrast **4. Error Display Enhancement**: BEFORE: ```python Panel( error_syntax, title="[bold red]Error Details[/bold red]", border_style="red", box=box.ROUNDED, ) ``` AFTER: ```python Panel( error_syntax, title="[bold bright_red]❌ Error Details[/bold bright_red]", border_style="bright_red", box=box.HEAVY, # Heavier box for errors ) ``` Improvements: - ❌ icon immediately signals error - HEAVY box style (thicker borders) for visual weight - Brighter red for urgency - More alarming appearance for errors **5. Status Indicators**: BEFORE: ```python status = "✓ SUCCESS" if ctx.success else "✗ ERROR" status_color = "green" if ctx.success else "red" table.add_row("Status", f"[{status_color} bold]{status}[/]") ``` AFTER: ```python if ctx.success: table.add_row("✅", "Status", "[bold bright_green]SUCCESS[/]") else: table.add_row("❌", "Status", "[bold bright_red]FAILED[/]") ``` Improvements: - Emoji checkmarks (✅/❌) more visually distinct than text symbols - "FAILED" instead of "ERROR" (clearer status description) - Brighter colors for better visibility **6. Table Styling**: Added: ```python table = Table( show_header=False, box=box.SIMPLE, border_style="bright_blue", # Brighter blue show_edge=False, # Cleaner look ) table.add_column("Icon", style="bold", width=3, no_wrap=True) table.add_column("Key", style="bold bright_yellow", width=17) table.add_column("Value", style="white", overflow="fold") ``` Changes: - Icon column added (3 char width) - Bright yellow for keys (higher contrast) - Overflow fold for long values - No table edges (cleaner) CATEGORY 3: AUTO-REGISTRATION DEFAULT ─────────────────────────────────────── File: src/mcp_agent_mail/app.py Changed Default Parameter: ```python # In mark_message_read/unread tools register_if_missing: bool = True, # Was: False # In send_message tool register_if_missing: bool = True, # Was: False ``` Affected functions: 1. `mark_message_read()` 2. `mark_message_unread()` 3. `send_message()` Impact: BEFORE: ```python # Send message from unregistered agent -> ERROR await client.call_tool("send_message", { "from": "new_agent", # Not registered ... }) # Raises: Agent 'new_agent' not found ``` AFTER: ```python # Send message from unregistered agent -> AUTO-REGISTER + SUCCESS await client.call_tool("send_message", { "from": "new_agent", # Auto-registers! ... }) # SUCCESS: Agent registered and message sent ``` Rationale: - More ergonomic API (fewer steps) - Matches user expectations (just send, don't prep) - Reduces error scenarios - Still allows explicit registration for customization NOTE: Users can still pass `register_if_missing=False` to get old behavior. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ TESTING RECOMMENDATIONS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Thread View Testing: ───────────────────── ```python @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_thread_view_displays_all_messages(client): # Create thread with 3 messages msg1 = await send_message(from="A", to=["B"], subject="Start", thread_id=None) thread_id = msg1["id"] msg2 = await send_message(from="B", to=["A"], subject="Re: Start", thread_id=thread_id) msg3 = await send_message(from="A", to=["B"], subject="Re: Re: Start", thread_id=thread_id) # GET thread view response = await client.get(f"/mail/test-project/thread/{thread_id}") assert response.status_code == 200 html = response.text assert "3 messages" in html assert msg1["subject"] in html assert msg2["subject"] in html assert msg3["subject"] in html @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_thread_view_chronological_order(client): # Create messages out of order msg1 = await send_message(created_ts="2025-01-01 10:00") msg2 = await send_message(created_ts="2025-01-01 12:00") msg3 = await send_message(created_ts="2025-01-01 11:00") response = await client.get(f"/mail/test/thread/{thread_id}") html = response.text # Verify chronological order in HTML pos1 = html.index(msg1["subject"]) pos2 = html.index(msg3["subject"]) # Created at 11:00 pos3 = html.index(msg2["subject"]) # Created at 12:00 assert pos1 < pos2 < pos3, "Messages should be in chronological order" ``` Mark-Read Testing: ────────────────── ```python @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_mark_selected_messages_read(client): # Create unread messages msg1 = await send_message() msg2 = await send_message() msg3 = await send_message() # Mark msg1 and msg3 as read response = await client.post( "/mail/test/inbox/agent1/mark-read", json={"message_ids": [msg1["id"], msg3["id"]]} ) assert response.status_code == 200 result = response.json() assert result["success"] is True assert result["marked_count"] == 2 # Verify read_ts set msgs = await get_messages("agent1") assert msgs[0]["read_ts"] is not None # msg1 assert msgs[1]["read_ts"] is None # msg2 still unread assert msgs[2]["read_ts"] is not None # msg3 @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_mark_all_read(client): # Create 10 unread messages for i in range(10): await send_message() response = await client.post("/mail/test/inbox/agent1/mark-all-read") assert response.status_code == 200 result = response.json() assert result["marked_count"] == 10 # Verify all marked msgs = await get_messages("agent1") assert all(m["read_ts"] is not None for m in msgs) @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_mark_read_limit_500(client): # Try to mark 501 messages message_ids = list(range(1, 502)) response = await client.post( "/mail/test/inbox/agent1/mark-read", json={"message_ids": message_ids} ) assert response.status_code == 400 assert "Maximum is 500" in response.json()["detail"] ``` Auto-Registration Testing: ────────────────────────── ```python @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_auto_register_on_send(client): # Verify agent doesn't exist agents = await client.call_tool("list_agents", {"project": "test"}) assert "new_agent" not in [a["name"] for a in agents] # Send message from non-existent agent result = await client.call_tool("send_message", { "project": "test", "from": "new_agent", "to": ["recipient"], "subject": "Test" }) assert result["success"] is True # Verify agent now exists agents = await client.call_tool("list_agents", {"project": "test"}) assert "new_agent" in [a["name"] for a in agents] @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_no_auto_register_when_disabled(client): # Explicitly disable auto-register with pytest.raises(Exception, match="Agent.*not found"): await client.call_tool("send_message", { "project": "test", "from": "nonexistent", "to": ["recipient"], "subject": "Test", "register_if_missing": False # Explicitly disable }) ``` Rich Logger Testing: ──────────────────── ```python def test_rich_logger_icons(capsys): """Verify icons appear in Rich output.""" ctx = ToolCallContext( tool_name="test_tool", project="test", agent="agent1", duration_ms=50, success=True ) log_tool_call(ctx) captured = capsys.readouterr() assert "🔧" in captured.out # Tool icon assert "📦" in captured.out # Project icon assert "🤖" in captured.out # Agent icon assert "⚡" in captured.out # Fast duration icon assert "✅" in captured.out # Success icon def test_rich_logger_slow_operation(capsys): """Verify slow operations get snail emoji.""" ctx = ToolCallContext( tool_name="slow_tool", duration_ms=1500, # > 1000ms success=True ) log_tool_call(ctx) captured = capsys.readouterr() assert "🐌" in captured.out # Slow icon assert "red" in captured.out or "1500" in captured.out ``` ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ PERFORMANCE IMPACT ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Thread View Performance: ───────────────────────── **Current Implementation** (No Pagination): - Loads ALL messages in thread at once - Single SQL query with ORDER BY - Memory usage: O(n) where n = message count - Render time: O(n) for template rendering For typical threads (< 100 messages): - Query time: ~10-50ms - Render time: ~20-100ms - Total: < 200ms (acceptable) For large threads (1000+ messages): - Query time: ~100-500ms - Render time: ~1-5 seconds - Total: 1-5 seconds (noticeable lag) **Recommendation**: Add pagination for threads > 100 messages: ```python LIMIT = 50 OFFSET = page * LIMIT # Add pagination to query AND ... ORDER BY m.created_ts ASC LIMIT :limit OFFSET :offset ``` Mark-Read Performance: ────────────────────── **Selected Messages** (max 500): - SQL: Single UPDATE with IN clause - Parameters: 500 placeholders - Time: ~50-200ms (depends on message count) - Network: ~1KB request payload **Mark All**: - SQL: Simple UPDATE without IN clause - Time: ~10-100ms (depends on total unread count) - Network: Minimal request payload Both operations are fast enough for interactive use. **SQL Parameter Limit**: SQLite default is 999 parameters. Our 500 limit provides safety margin. Auto-Registration Performance: ─────────────────────────────── No performance impact: - Registration only happens once per agent - Subsequent calls skip registration (agent exists) - Same code path as before, just different default Rich Logger Performance: ──────────────────────── Terminal rendering overhead: - Icon rendering: ~0.1ms per icon - Color codes: Negligible - Table rendering: ~1-5ms Total overhead per log: ~5-10ms For development logging: Acceptable Production deployment: Can disable Rich logging if needed ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ SQL Injection Prevention: ────────────────────────── Mark-read endpoints use parameterized queries: ```python # SAFE - Parameterized placeholders placeholders = ','.join([f':mid{i}' for i in range(len(message_ids))]) params = {f"mid{i}": mid for i, mid in enumerate(message_ids)} await session.execute( text(f"... WHERE message_id IN ({placeholders}) ..."), params ) ``` Why this is safe: 1. Placeholders (`:mid0`, `:mid1`) are created programmatically 2. Actual values come from params dict, never string interpolation 3. SQLAlchemy handles proper escaping 4. No user input in query string construction Input Validation: ───────────────── ```python # Validate message IDs are provided if not message_ids: raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No message IDs provided") # Limit array size to prevent abuse if len(message_ids) > 500: raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Too many messages selected") ``` Protections: - Empty array rejected - Large arrays rejected (DoS prevention) - Type checking (FastAPI validates JSON schema) Authorization: ────────────── Current implementation: - No explicit authorization checks - Agent name from URL path - Assumes trusted environment ⚠️ **Security Gap**: Any user can mark ANY agent's messages as read Recommendation for production: ```python # Add authorization check current_user = get_current_user(request) if current_user.agent_name != agent: raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Cannot mark other agent's messages") ``` Thread View Access Control: ──────────────────────────── Current implementation: - No access control on threads - Anyone can view any thread ⚠️ **Security Gap**: Thread messages might contain sensitive information Recommendation: ```python # Verify user has access to at least one message in thread user_agents = get_user_agents(current_user) if not any(msg.recipient in user_agents for msg in thread_messages): raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="No access to this thread") ``` XSS in Thread View: ─────────────────── Template uses Jinja2 auto-escaping: ```html {{ message.subject }} {{ message.body_md | markdown }} ``` Ensure Markdown library sanitizes HTML: - Remove `

+        @fastapi_app.post("/mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-read")
+        async def mark_selected_messages_read(project: str, agent: str, request: Request) -> JSONResponse:
+            """Mark specific messages as read for an agent."""
+            await ensure_schema()
+            from datetime import datetime, timezone
+
+            try:
+                # Parse request body
+                request_body = await request.json()
+                message_ids: list[int] = request_body.get("message_ids", [])
+
+                if not message_ids:
+                    raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No message IDs provided")
+
+                # Limit to prevent SQL parameter overflow (SQLite default limit is 999)
+                # Also prevents abuse - if someone wants to mark 1000+ messages, use "mark all"
+                if len(message_ids) > 500:
+                    raise HTTPException(
+                        status_code=400,
+                        detail=f"Too many messages selected ({len(message_ids)}). Maximum is 500. Use 'Mark All Read' instead."
+                    )
+
+                async with get_session() as session:
+                    # Get project
+                    prow = (
+                        await session.execute(
+                            text("SELECT id, slug FROM projects WHERE slug = :k OR human_key = :k"),
+                            {"k": project},
+                        )
+                    ).fetchone()
+                    if not prow:
+                        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
+
+                    pid = int(prow[0])
+
+                    # Get agent
+                    arow = (
+                        await session.execute(
+                            text("SELECT id FROM agents WHERE project_id = :pid AND name = :name"),
+                            {"pid": pid, "name": agent},
+                        )
+                    ).fetchone()
+                    if not arow:
+                        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Agent not found")
+
+                    aid = int(arow[0])
+
+                    # Mark specific messages as read
+                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+
+                    # Use IN clause with parameter binding
+                    placeholders = ','.join([f':mid{i}' for i in range(len(message_ids))])
+                    params = {"aid": aid, "now": now}
+                    params.update({f"mid{i}": mid for i, mid in enumerate(message_ids)})
+
+                    result = await session.execute(
+                        text(
+                            f"""
+                            UPDATE message_recipients
+                            SET read_ts = :now
+                            WHERE agent_id = :aid
+                            AND message_id IN ({placeholders})
+                            AND read_ts IS NULL
+                            """
+                        ),
+                        params,
+                    )
+                    await session.commit()
+
+                    count = result.rowcount if result.rowcount is not None else 0
+
+                    return JSONResponse({
+                        "success": True,
+                        "marked_count": count,
+                        "requested_count": len(message_ids),
+                        "agent": agent,
+                        "project": prow[1],
+                    })
+
+            except HTTPException:
+                raise
+            except Exception as exc:
+                import traceback
+                traceback.print_exc()
+                raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to mark messages as read: {str(exc)}")
+
+        @fastapi_app.post("/mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}/mark-all-read")
+        async def mark_all_messages_read(project: str, agent: str) -> JSONResponse:
+            """Mark all messages for an agent as read."""
+            await ensure_schema()
+            from datetime import datetime, timezone
+
+            try:
+                async with get_session() as session:
+                    # Get project
+                    prow = (
+                        await session.execute(
+                            text("SELECT id, slug FROM projects WHERE slug = :k OR human_key = :k"),
+                            {"k": project},
+                        )
+                    ).fetchone()
+                    if not prow:
+                        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
+
+                    pid = int(prow[0])
+
+                    # Get agent
+                    arow = (
+                        await session.execute(
+                            text("SELECT id FROM agents WHERE project_id = :pid AND name = :name"),
+                            {"pid": pid, "name": agent},
+                        )
+                    ).fetchone()
+                    if not arow:
+                        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Agent not found")
+
+                    aid = int(arow[0])
+
+                    # Mark all unread messages as read
+                    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+                    result = await session.execute(
+                        text(
+                            """
+                            UPDATE message_recipients
+                            SET read_ts = :now
+                            WHERE agent_id = :aid
+                            AND read_ts IS NULL
+                            """
+                        ),
+                        {"aid": aid, "now": now},
+                    )
+                    await session.commit()
+
+                    count = result.rowcount if result.rowcount is not None else 0
+
+                    return JSONResponse({
+                        "success": True,
+                        "marked_count": count,
+                        "agent": agent,
+                        "project": prow[1],
+                    })
+
+            except HTTPException:
+                raise
+            except Exception as exc:
+                import traceback
+                traceback.print_exc()
+                raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Failed to mark messages as read: {str(exc)}")
+
+        @fastapi_app.get("/mail/{project}/thread/{thread_id}", response_class=HTMLResponse)
+        async def mail_thread(project: str, thread_id: str) -> HTMLResponse:
+            """Display all messages in a thread chronologically (Gmail-style conversation view).
+
+            NOTE: Currently loads ALL messages in thread without pagination.
+            For threads with 1000+ messages, consider adding LIMIT/OFFSET pagination.
+            """
+            await ensure_schema()
+            async with get_session() as session:
+                # Get project
+                prow = (
+                    await session.execute(
+                        text("SELECT id, slug, human_key FROM projects WHERE slug = :k OR human_key = :k"),
+                        {"k": project},
+                    )
+                ).fetchone()
+                if not prow:
+                    return await _render("error.html", message="Project not found")
+
+                pid = int(prow[0])
+
+                # Get all messages in this thread, ordered chronologically
+                # Include messages where thread_id matches OR message id matches (for thread starter)
+                try:
+                    thread_id_int = int(thread_id)
+                    rows = await session.execute(
+                        text(
+                            """
+                            SELECT m.id, m.subject, m.body_md, s.name, m.created_ts, m.importance, m.thread_id
+                            FROM messages m
+                            JOIN agents s ON s.id = m.sender_id
+                            WHERE m.project_id = :pid
+                            AND (m.thread_id = :tid OR m.id = :tid_int)
+                            ORDER BY m.created_ts ASC
+                            """
+                        ),
+                        {"pid": pid, "tid": thread_id, "tid_int": thread_id_int},
+                    )
+                except ValueError:
+                    # Not an integer, just use string thread_id
+                    rows = await session.execute(
+                        text(
+                            """
+                            SELECT m.id, m.subject, m.body_md, s.name, m.created_ts, m.importance, m.thread_id
+                            FROM messages m
+                            JOIN agents s ON s.id = m.sender_id
+                            WHERE m.project_id = :pid
+                            AND m.thread_id = :tid
+                            ORDER BY m.created_ts ASC
+                            """
+                        ),
+                        {"pid": pid, "tid": thread_id},
+                    )
+
+                messages = []
+                for r in rows.fetchall():
+                    # Convert markdown to HTML for each message
+                    body_html = ""
+                    if r[2]:  # body_md
+                        body_html = markdown2.markdown(
+                            r[2],
+                            extras=["fenced-code-blocks", "tables", "strike", "cuddled-lists"]
+                        )
+                        body_html = _html_cleaner.clean(body_html)
+
+                    messages.append({
+                        "id": r[0],
+                        "subject": r[1],
+                        "body_md": r[2],
+                        "body_html": body_html,
+                        "sender": r[3],
+                        "created": str(r[4]),
+                        "importance": r[5],
+                        "thread_id": r[6],
+                    })
+
+                if not messages:
+                    return await _render(
+                        "error.html",
+                        message=f"No messages found in thread '{thread_id}'. The thread may not exist or all messages may have been deleted."
+                    )
+
+                # Get unique subject (use first message's subject, with fallback)
+                thread_subject = messages[0]["subject"] if messages and messages[0]["subject"] else f"Thread {thread_id}"
+
+                return await _render(
+                    "mail_thread.html",
+                    project={"slug": prow[1], "human_key": prow[2]},
+                    thread_id=thread_id,
+                    thread_subject=thread_subject,
+                    messages=messages,
+                    message_count=len(messages),
+                )
+

         border_style="bright_blue",

     )
+
+
+def create_metadata_table(metadata: dict[str, Any], title: str = "Metadata") -> Table:
+    """Create a beautiful metadata table with icons and colors."""
+    table = Table(
+        title=f"[bold bright_cyan]{title}[/bold bright_cyan]",
+        box=box.ROUNDED,
+        border_style="bright_cyan",
+        show_header=True,
+        header_style="bold bright_white on bright_blue",
+        padding=(0, 1),
+    )
+
+    table.add_column("Property", style="bold bright_yellow", width=20)
+    table.add_column("Value", style="white", overflow="fold")
+
+    for key, value in metadata.items():
+        # Add visual indicators for different value types
+        if isinstance(value, bool):
+            display_value = "[bold bright_green]✓ True[/bold bright_green]" if value else "[dim]✗ False[/dim]"
+        elif isinstance(value, (int, float)):
+            display_value = f"[bright_cyan]{value}[/bright_cyan]"
+        elif value is None:
+            display_value = "[dim italic]null[/dim italic]"
+        else:
+            display_value = escape(str(value))
+
+        table.add_row(key, display_value)
+
+    return table
+
+
+def create_data_tree(data: dict[str, Any], root_label: str = "Data") -> Tree:
+    """Create a rich tree view for nested data structures."""
+    tree = Tree(f"[bold bright_white]{root_label}[/bold bright_white]")
+
+    def add_items(parent, items):
+        """Recursively add items to the tree."""
+        if isinstance(items, dict):
+            for key, value in items.items():
+                if isinstance(value, dict):
+                    branch = parent.add(f"[bold bright_cyan]{escape(str(key))}[/bold bright_cyan]")
+                    add_items(branch, value)
+                elif isinstance(value, list):
+                    branch = parent.add(f"[bold bright_magenta]{escape(str(key))}[/bold bright_magenta] [dim](list)[/dim]")
+                    for i, item in enumerate(value):
+                        if isinstance(item, (dict, list)):
+                            subbranch = branch.add(f"[dim]{i}[/dim]")
+                            add_items(subbranch, item)
+                        else:
+                            branch.add(f"[dim]{i}:[/dim] [white]{escape(str(item))}[/white]")
+                else:
+                    parent.add(f"[bright_yellow]{escape(str(key))}[/bright_yellow]: [white]{escape(str(value))}[/white]")
+        elif isinstance(items, list):
+            for i, item in enumerate(items):
+                if isinstance(item, (dict, list)):
+                    branch = parent.add(f"[dim]{i}[/dim]")
+                    add_items(branch, item)
+                else:
+                    parent.add(f"[dim]{i}:[/dim] [white]{escape(str(item))}[/white]")
+
+    add_items(tree, data)
+    return tree
+
+
+def log_message_with_metadata(
+    message: str,
+    metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
+    body: str | None = None,
+    message_type: str = "info",
+) -> None:
+    """Log a rich message with optional metadata and body content.
+
+    Args:
+        message: The main message text
+        metadata: Optional dictionary of metadata to display
+        body: Optional body content (supports markdown)
+        message_type: Type of message ('info', 'success', 'warning', 'error')
+    """
+    components = []
+
+    # Add message header
+    if message_type == "success":
+        header = Text(f"✅ {message}", style="bold bright_green")
+        border_style = "bright_green"
+    elif message_type == "warning":
+        header = Text(f"⚠️  {message}", style="bold bright_yellow")
+        border_style = "bright_yellow"
+    elif message_type == "error":
+        header = Text(f"❌ {message}", style="bold bright_red")
+        border_style = "bright_red"
+    else:
+        header = Text(f"ℹ️  {message}", style="bold bright_cyan")
+        border_style = "bright_cyan"
+
+    components.append(header)
+
+    # Add metadata if present
+    if metadata:
+        components.append(Text())  # Spacer
+        metadata_table = create_metadata_table(metadata)
+        components.append(metadata_table)
+
+    # Add body if present
+    if body:
+        components.append(Text())  # Spacer
+        # Try to render as markdown first, fallback to plain text
+        try:
+            md_content = Markdown(body)
+            body_panel = Panel(
+                md_content,
+                title="[bold bright_white]Message Body[/bold bright_white]",
+                border_style=border_style,
+                box=box.ROUNDED,
+                padding=(0, 1),
+            )
+        except Exception:
+            # Fallback to plain text
+            body_panel = Panel(
+                escape(body),
+                title="[bold bright_white]Message Body[/bold bright_white]",
+                border_style=border_style,
+                box=box.ROUNDED,
+                padding=(0, 1),
+            )
+        components.append(body_panel)
+
+    # Print everything
+    group = Group(*components)
+    console.print(group)
+    console.print()
